description: SDK Usability Benchmark CLI — measures how well AI agents can use an SDK by generating programming problems, running agents in sandboxed environments, and scoring with token analysis + LLM judge

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and dependency setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the TypeScript project initialized with all tooling so subsequent stories can add code to a working project.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json with name 'agentic-usability', type 'module', bin entry pointing to dist/index.js
Dependencies installed: commander, chalk, ora, cli-table3, turndown, @alibaba-group/opensandbox
Dev dependencies: typescript, @types/node, @types/turndown, eslint, tsx
tsconfig.json with target ES2022, module NodeNext, moduleResolution NodeNext, outDir dist, rootDir src, strict true
ESLint config for TypeScript
Directory structure created: src/commands/, src/core/, src/agents/, src/sandbox/, src/scoring/, src/reporting/
npm run build compiles without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Core TypeScript types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all shared TypeScript interfaces defined in src/core/types.ts so other modules can import them.
acceptanceCriteria: SolutionFile interface: { path: string, content: string }

TestCase interface: { id, problemStatement, referenceSolution: SolutionFile[], difficulty: 'easy'|'medium'|'hard', targetApis: string[], expectedTokens: string[], tags: string[], setupInstructions?: string }
AgentResult interface: { stdout, stderr, exitCode: number, durationMs: number }
TokenResult interface: { token, found: boolean, foundIn?: string }
TokenAnalysis interface: { testId, target, apis: TokenResult[], tokens: TokenResult[], apiCoverage: number, tokenCoverage: number }
JudgeScore interface: { testId, target, functionalEquivalence, apiCorrectness, idiomaticUsage, overallSimilarity: number, functionalMatch: boolean, notes: string }

Config interface matching the .agentic-usability.json schema: source (type local|git|url, path, url, urls, branch, subpath, sparse), publicInfo (docsUrl, guides, packageName, installCommand, additionalContext), agents (generator, executor, judge each with command+args), targets array (name, image, timeout), workspace (template, setupScript, env), sandbox (domain, apiKey, concurrency, defaultTimeout, systemPrompt), output (dir, suiteFile)
PipelineState interface: { stage, startedAt, testCases: number, completed: { generate, execute, analyze, judge } }
All types exported from src/core/types.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Config loader and validator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a module that loads and validates .agentic-usability.json so all commands can access typed configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: src/core/config.ts exports loadConfig() that reads .agentic-usability.json from cwd
Validates required fields: source.type, at least one target, sandbox.domain
Validates source.type is one of: 'local', 'git', 'url'
For source.type 'local', requires source.path; for 'git', requires source.url; for 'url', requires source.urls array
Returns typed Config object from src/core/types.ts
Throws descriptive error if config file is missing or invalid
ensureWorkingDir() creates .agentic-usability/ directory if it doesn't exist
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CLI entry point with subcommand stubs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the CLI entry point with commander so all commands are registered and routable.
acceptanceCriteria: src/index.ts uses commander to define the CLI program with name 'agentic-usability'
Subcommands registered as stubs: init, generate, execute, analyze, judge, report, run, export, import, edit
Each stub prints 'Not implemented yet' and exits
--help shows all subcommands with descriptions
--version reads version from package.json
Shebang line (#!/usr/bin/env node) at top of entry file
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Agent adapter interface and built-in adapters
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pluggable agent adapter system so any CLI-based AI coding agent can be used.
acceptanceCriteria: src/agents/adapter.ts exports AgentAdapter interface: { name: string, execute(prompt: string, workDir: string, env?: Record<string, string>): Promise<AgentResult> }
src/agents/claude.ts implements ClaudeAdapter — spawns 'claude --print -p <prompt>' with --workdir flag
src/agents/codex.ts implements CodexAdapter — spawns codex CLI with appropriate flags
src/agents/gemini.ts implements GeminiAdapter — spawns gemini-cli with appropriate flags
src/agents/custom.ts implements CustomAdapter — takes command template from config, interpolates {prompt} and {workDir}
All adapters use child_process.spawn, capture stdout/stderr, measure durationMs, enforce a configurable timeout
createAdapter(agentConfig) factory function returns the right adapter based on config.command
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Source resolver for local and git types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a source resolver that gives the test generation agent a local working directory from local paths or git repos.
acceptanceCriteria: src/core/source-resolver.ts exports resolveSource(config: Config): Promise<string> returning absolute path
For type 'local': validates path exists, appends subpath if set, returns absolute path
For type 'git': clones to .agentic-usability/repos/<url-hash>/ with 'git clone --depth 1 --branch <branch>'
Git sparse checkout supported: if config.source.sparse is set, only checkout those paths
Caches git clones — skips clone if directory exists unless --fresh flag is passed
Clear error messages: 'Directory not found: /path' or 'Git clone failed: <stderr>'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Source resolver for URL type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the source resolver to handle URL sources by fetching web pages and converting them to local markdown files.
acceptanceCriteria: For source type 'url': fetches each URL in config.source.urls using native fetch()
HTML responses converted to markdown using turndown library
Converted files saved to .agentic-usability/repos/url-<hash>/ with filename derived from URL
Non-HTML responses (plain text, markdown) saved as-is
Caches fetched URLs — skips fetch if files exist unless --fresh flag is passed
Returns the directory path so the test generation agent can explore it
Error handling: logs warning for unreachable URLs but continues with remaining URLs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Init command with interactive prompts
userStories.description: As an SDK author, I want to run 'agentic-usability init' to create a config file through guided prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces the init stub in src/commands/init.ts
Prompts for source type (local/git/url) and corresponding path/url/urls
Prompts for publicInfo: docsUrl, packageName, installCommand
Prompts for agent command (default: 'claude')
Prompts for at least one target: name and Docker image
Prompts for sandbox domain (default: 'localhost:8080')
Writes .agentic-usability.json with collected values
Creates .agentic-usability/ working directory
Validates local paths exist before writing config
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use commander's built-in prompt or a library like inquirer/prompts for interactive input

userStories.title: Test suite generation command
userStories.description: As an SDK author, I want 'agentic-usability generate' to have an AI agent explore my SDK source and produce a test suite.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces the generate stub in src/commands/generate.ts
Loads config, resolves source to a local path using resolveSource()
Creates the generator agent via createAdapter(config.agents.generator)
Sends structured prompt instructing the agent to: explore the codebase, identify public APIs, generate TestCase[] JSON
Prompt specifies output format matching TestCase interface including referenceSolution as SolutionFile[]
Parses agent stdout as JSON, validates each test case has required fields
Auto-generates sequential IDs (TC-001, TC-002, etc.) if not provided by agent
Saves validated suite to the path in config.output.suiteFile (default: .agentic-usability/suite.json)
Prints summary: count of test cases, difficulty breakdown, target APIs found
Retries once with correction prompt if agent output is malformed JSON
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Test suite review, export, and import commands
userStories.description: As an SDK author, I want to review, edit, export, and import test suites for manual curation and sharing.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces edit, export, and import stubs
'agentic-usability edit' opens suite.json in $EDITOR (falls back to vi), re-validates on save
'agentic-usability export --output <path>' copies suite.json to specified path
'agentic-usability import --input <path>' loads and validates a test suite file, copies to suite.json location
Import validates JSON against TestCase[] schema with per-field error messages
Import prompts for confirmation before overwriting existing suite
After generation or import, prints summary table: ID, difficulty, problem statement (first 60 chars), targetApis count
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: OpenSandbox client wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a wrapper around the @alibaba-group/opensandbox SDK that simplifies sandbox lifecycle for our use case.
acceptanceCriteria: src/sandbox/opensandbox.ts exports SandboxClient class
Constructor takes sandbox config (domain, apiKey) and creates ConnectionConfig
create(image, env, timeout) method creates a sandbox and waits for Running state with polling (max 60s)
uploadFiles(files: Array<{path, data}>) uploads files via sandbox.files.writeFiles()
runCommand(cmd: string) executes command and returns { stdout, stderr, exitCode }
listFiles(path: string) lists files in a directory
readFile(path: string) reads a file's content
destroy() calls sandbox.kill() and sandbox.close()
checkConnectivity() static method that tests if the OpenSandbox server is reachable; throws descriptive error with setup instructions if not
All OpenSandbox errors wrapped with actionable messages
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Public docs fetcher and cacher
userStories.description: As a developer, I need public documentation fetched, cached, and assembled into DOCS.md for sandbox agents.
acceptanceCriteria: src/sandbox/docs-fetcher.ts exports fetchAndCacheDocs(publicInfo: PublicInfo): Promise<string>
Fetches publicInfo.docsUrl and each URL in publicInfo.guides using native fetch()
HTML responses converted to markdown using turndown
Caches fetched docs in .agentic-usability/cache/docs/ with URL-hash filenames
Cache TTL: skips fetch if cached file is less than 24 hours old
Assembles single DOCS.md string from all fetched docs + publicInfo.additionalContext
Truncates to 100KB with a note: '... Full documentation at <url>'
Accepts --fresh-docs flag to bypass cache
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Workspace scaffolding for sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the 4-layer workspace scaffolding system that prepares a sandbox before the AI agent starts.
acceptanceCriteria: src/sandbox/scaffolding.ts exports scaffoldWorkspace(client: SandboxClient, config: Config, testCase: TestCase): Promise<void>
Layer 2 (Template): if config.workspace.template is set, recursively reads all files from that local directory and uploads to /workspace/ via client.uploadFiles()
Layer 3 (Global Setup): if config.workspace.setupScript is set, uploads the script and executes it; fails test case if exit code is non-zero
Layer 4 (Per-Test Setup): if testCase.setupInstructions is set, executes those commands in the sandbox
Each layer is optional — function works with none, some, or all configured
Logs setup output to a string that callers can save as setup.log
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Layer 1 (container image) is handled by SandboxClient.create() — not part of this story

userStories.title: Sandbox test executor and solution extractor
userStories.description: As an SDK author, I want 'agentic-usability execute' to run each test case in an OpenSandbox container with an AI agent and extract the generated solution.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces the execute stub in src/commands/execute.ts
For each test case: creates sandbox via SandboxClient → scaffolds workspace → uploads PROBLEM.md + DOCS.md → installs agent CLI → runs agent → extracts solution → destroys sandbox
PROBLEM.md contains testCase.problemStatement; DOCS.md assembled via docs fetcher
Agent CLI installed inside sandbox via runCommand (e.g., 'npm i -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code')
Agent invoked with system prompt from config.sandbox.systemPrompt with {{packageName}} and {{docsUrl}} interpolated
Solution extraction: lists files in /workspace/solution/, reads each file, builds SolutionFile[] JSON
Saves generated-solution.json and agent-output.log to .agentic-usability/results/<test-id>/
Sandbox destroyed after extraction completes
Validates OpenSandbox connectivity before starting (SandboxClient.checkConnectivity())
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Concurrency is handled separately in US-015. This story runs test cases sequentially.

userStories.title: Execution concurrency manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a worker pool that runs sandbox executions in parallel with configurable concurrency.
acceptanceCriteria: src/sandbox/worker-pool.ts exports WorkerPool class
Constructor takes concurrency limit (from config.sandbox.concurrency, default 3)
run(testCases: TestCase[], executeFn) processes the queue with N parallel workers
Each worker calls executeFn(testCase) which is the single-test-case executor from US-014
Failed sandbox creations retry up to 2 times with exponential backoff (1s, 3s)
Progress callback reports: completed, running, queued, failed counts
Graceful shutdown on SIGINT: finish running workers, skip queued items
execute command updated to use WorkerPool instead of sequential execution
Progress displayed in terminal: '[3/20] TC-003 (medium) — running...' with elapsed time
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Code token analysis command
userStories.description: As an SDK author, I want 'agentic-usability analyze' to check generated solutions for expected SDK API calls and patterns using regex.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces the analyze stub in src/commands/analyze.ts
src/scoring/tokens.ts exports analyzeTokens(solution: SolutionFile[], targetApis: string[], expectedTokens: string[]): TokenAnalysis
targetApis matched with word-boundary regex (\b) to avoid false positives
expectedTokens matched with full regex support (e.g., 'import.*my-sdk')
Each token result includes: token string, found boolean, foundIn file path (first match)
apiCoverage = (found apis / total apis) * 100; tokenCoverage = (found tokens / total tokens) * 100
For each test case: loads generated-solution.json from results, runs analyzeTokens, saves token-analysis.json
Prints per-test summary: 'TC-001: API 100% (3/3), Tokens 85% (6/7)'
Missing solution files result in 0% coverage with a warning
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LLM judge comparison command
userStories.description: As an SDK author, I want 'agentic-usability judge' to have an LLM compare reference and generated solutions holistically.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces the judge stub in src/commands/judge.ts
src/scoring/judge.ts exports formatSolution(files: SolutionFile[]): string that renders files as '--- File: path ---\ncontent\n'
Judge prompt includes: problem statement, formatted reference solution, formatted generated solution
Judge prompt asks for JSON response with: functionalEquivalence, apiCorrectness, idiomaticUsage, overallSimilarity (0-100 each), functionalMatch (boolean), notes (string)
Invokes the judge agent via createAdapter(config.agents.judge)
Parses agent stdout as JSON, validates against JudgeScore schema
Retries once with correction prompt if output is malformed JSON
Saves judge.json to .agentic-usability/results/<test-id>/
Skippable via --skip-judge flag
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scorecard reporting and results export
userStories.description: As an SDK author, I want 'agentic-usability report' to display a terminal scorecard and optionally export results as JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces the report stub in src/commands/report.ts
Reads all results from .agentic-usability/results/ (token-analysis.json, judge.json, generated-solution.json per test)
Displays terminal table using cli-table3: Test ID, Difficulty, API Coverage, Token Coverage, Similarity, Problem (truncated)
Aggregate section: average API coverage, average token coverage, average similarity, breakdown by difficulty
Worst performing APIs section: targetApis ranked by miss rate across all solutions
Missed tokens section: expectedTokens ranked by miss rate
--json flag outputs raw structured JSON to stdout instead of the table
'agentic-usability export-results --output <path>' writes all results to a single JSON file
Handles missing results gracefully (shows 'N/A' for tests without results)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pipeline state persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pipeline state saved to disk so runs can be resumed after interruption.
acceptanceCriteria: src/core/pipeline.ts exports PipelineStateManager class
State stored in .agentic-usability/pipeline-state.json matching PipelineState interface
load() reads existing state or returns a fresh initial state
save() writes current state to disk
markTestComplete(stage, testId) updates per-test-case completion within a stage
advanceStage(stage) transitions to the next pipeline stage
isTestComplete(stage, testId): boolean checks if a specific test was already processed
getIncompleteTests(stage, allTestIds): string[] returns test IDs not yet completed for a stage
--fresh flag support: reset() clears existing state (with caller handling confirmation)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pipeline orchestration (run command)
userStories.description: As an SDK author, I want 'agentic-usability run' to execute the full pipeline end-to-end with resumability.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces the run stub in src/commands/run.ts
Executes stages in sequence: generate → execute → analyze → judge → report
Uses PipelineStateManager to track progress and skip completed work on --resume
Stage transitions displayed with chalk: '[Stage 2/5] Executing test cases...'
--skip-judge flag skips the judge stage (pipeline goes generate → execute → analyze → report)
--resume flag loads existing pipeline state and continues from last checkpoint
--fresh flag clears existing state after confirmation prompt
Errors in individual test cases logged but do not halt the pipeline
Final output is the scorecard (calls report logic)
Typecheck passes
